content.content:
# Masonry Layout Stacking Behavior

According to the MDN documentation, here is the exact verbatim description of how items behave on the stacking axis:

> "On the stacking axis, rather than sticking to a strict grid with gaps being left after shorter items, the items in the following row rise up to fill the gaps."

This is the key characteristic that distinguishes masonry layout from regular grid layout. Instead of maintaining strict row alignment where shorter items leave empty space below them, items in subsequent rows actively move upward to fill any gaps created by taller items in previous rows, resulting in a tightly packed layout similar to traditional brickwork/masonry.
